string 2:
This is an extradimensional space created by
a certain Digimon. It's called an "Akashic Backdoor."

Several space-time disturbances have been used to connect
this Akashic Backdoor to various points in space-time.

Your mission is to defeat the Digimon responsible for 
creating and maintaining this Akashic Backdoor.

Meanwhile, I'll be using your device to provide you with
updates as necessary. I look forward to working with you.

A new Akashic Backdoor...! It seems that same Digimon 
has once again warped the natural laws of the world.
You'll have to defeat them, just like you did before.
Fade into the dust of space-time!
Wh, what...?!
Th-This can't be good...!
Absorbent Bang!
Augh!

That was Parallelmon, the Digimon behind all this.
Thanks to some mutation, they're able to travel through space-time.

They seem to have built up Akashic Backdoors like a web,
and use them to slip from one point in space-time to another.

They can travel to the past or to the future,
but in doing so, they destabilize the world.

So this Akashic Backdoor has to be destroyed to prevent
the collapse of the fundamental laws of reality. Good luck!
As I feared, Parallelmon has created another Akashic Backdoor.
It has to be destroyed, just like before. Good luck!
Are you awake?

You seem to have gotten caught up in our battle,
to disastrous results. I do hope you are not injured.

But allow me to make the appropriate introductions.
I am Omnimon, and this is Dorumon.

So you got dragged into this world
because of Parallelmon, too, huh?
Well, greetings, fellow straggler!

That Digimon suddenly appeared before us and hit us
with that strange beam of light, "Absorbent Bang"...

The next thing we knew, we were transported to this
other world. Doubtless, it was the same for you.

We all wish to return to our original worlds. I propose that
we team up to defeat Parallelmon and make that happen.
Four other Digimon got sent here besides us.

If we can track down all four of them and fight as one,
we are sure to be more than a match for Parallelmon.
Yeah, IF we can "fight as one"...

In any case, the more allies we have, the better. We should
search for the other four Digimon we were separated from.

What do you say? Are you willing to lend us a hand?
I can assure you that we are quite trustworthy.
→End
What is this place?

Akashic Backdoors use space-time disturbances to
pry open pathways to different points in time and space.

This creates isolated fragments of space-time called
"Akashic debris," which is what you're looking at right now.

Ah, yes, I see...
What an intricate and baffling problem.

Asuna, what I'm about to ask is far from scientific, but 
please don't laugh... Would you pinch me on the cheek?

This is no dream.
I just pinched my thigh to make sure of that myself.
I see. In that case, let me ask...
You... Are you the same "you" that I know?

Never mind. I realize that was confusing.
I'm rather confused myself, actually.

I take it that Digimon—Parallelmon—hit you
with their beam of light as well?

If so, then you're in the same position as us. We suddenly
woke up to find ourselves in this strange, white space...
Then we opened a door and wound up here.
Those two probably come from a different timeline than you do.

That means the fundamental laws of reality we once took for
granted are breaking down because of Parallelmon. Not good.

...Well, since we all arrived here under similar circumstances,
I imagine we all share the same goal.
We have to defeat Parallelmon and get back to our world.

And to do that, we need to reunite with the four Digimon
we got separated from when we came here.

Yes, yes, understood. Teaming up sounds like a good idea. 
And, actually, we were separated from someone, too.
We'll need to drag that fool back with us as well.
Hey, everyone...

Could you come over here? You're not going to believe
what's on the other side of this door...
It's so bright. I can't see...
Hey, isn't this...?
It seems we are right back where we started.

Well, that's perfect, then.
It gives us a chance to start over.

Why don't you tell us about these four friends of yours?
How might we recognize them?
I'm... not sure if "friends" is the right word.

What we and the other four have in common is that we 
harbor a substance in our bodies known as "the X Antibody."

Yet the principles we believe in could not be more different...
As a result, we have split into three different groups.

Given the circumstances, however, it may be possible
for us to set our differences aside and fight as one.

Yeah, exactly.
I... really hope we can do that.

So, having explored this place a bit before we met you,
I've come up with a few theories based on what we learned.

We're probably from different timelines. That's the only
way to account for the discrepancies in our stories.

But, to verify that, would you mind telling us
a little more about the world you come from?
Hmm... Where to begin?

Dorumon and I have been searching for a means
to counteract something called "the X Program."

The X Program can also be called
the “Digimon Elimination Program.”

Digimon... elimination?
That doesn't exactly sound pleasant.

In our world, the rapid growth in the number of Digimon
far outpaced any and all expectations.

The vast amount of data consumed the Digital World's resources—
at a rate ultimately believed to be unsustainable.

Then, one day, the X Program was suddenly put into place,
as if to cull the outsized Digimon population.
The X Program made so many Digimon disappear...
Only the ones who managed to gain antibodies survived.
Omnimon and I are two of these X Antibody Digimon.

Now, though the resource crisis has been averted,
the X Program continues to ravage the Digimon population.

Some see this as a travesty that demands action, while others
believe the X Program should be allowed to run its course.

A meeting between the two sides was set to take place,
but then we found ourselves transferred here.

I see. So, basically, you've been divided by practical and
ideological differences concerning this X Program.

Yes, that's the gist of it. The division continues to run
deep, and each group now acts entirely on its own.

Division from within, huh? Sounds like an old,
familiar story, wouldn't you say, Asuna?

I wouldn't even call it "old."
It wasn't that long ago that we left the organization.

No...? So much has happened since then; it feels like
ancient history. Anyway, the real point is...

If even WE could rejoin our old organization, there's hope 
these Digimon can fight together despite their differences.
Is that... a fight between Digimon?
Yah!
Tah!
It doesn't look like much of a fair fight...
Enough! This battle is clearly over.

Oh, yeah? And why should we hold back?
THEY attacked US first.

Besides... there are too many Digimon in the world.
It can't be helped that some aren't fit to survive.

This one doesn't possess the X Antibody.
It could never hope to defeat us "chosen" Digimon.

It seems these two will not listen to reason.
We will have to stop them by force.

Let that be a lesson to the lot of you! Now, calm down.
The circumstances in this world are nothing like our own.

I hold that the resource crisis due to the burgeoning
Digimon population will eventually spread to all worlds.

Therefore, the best thing to do is to nip the problem in the bud.
Parallelmon's existence may be a blessing in disguise.
What a disturbing thing to say.

They view the X Program as a boon, and believe
it should be embraced as part of natural selection.

As they see it, since only the strong will survive, this will
bring about the next evolutionary step for the species.

They think they're so special.
Boy, does that rub me the wrong way!

The idea that selection and evolution go hand in hand
may be extreme, but there is some logic behind it.

However, whether something is logically coherent and
whether it is an optimal solution are seperate matters altogether.

If they believe their way is the one true way... they still
have a lot to learn. Cooperating with them won't be easy.

Stop this! We're all Digimon here.
What purpose will it serve for us to fight one another?

Exactly! We want to save all the Digimon,
even those without the X Antibody!

This is like the reverse of what we saw before.
These Digimon are trying to call for an end to the fighting.
But that leaves them completely on the defensive.
We should lend them a hand!

Are you all right?
I do hope you are not injured.

Why did you harm them?!
Was it because of your prejudice toward them?!
*sigh* I was afraid of this.
Afraid of what?
These are Digimon who oppose the X Program.

Frankly, it is hard to imagine there are many in favor of
a program that threatens to render our kind extinct.

It is not only the X Program that we oppose. We are against
any form of violence between Digimon rooted in prejudice.

So... how is your thinking any different from
that of Omnimon and Dorumon here?

They seem to believe something needs to be done
about the X Program, too...
No. The two of them are merely cowards.
Hey! Who're you calling a coward?!

You have the X Antibody, which means you lived through
that tragedy. It's your duty as a survivor to end the program!

There's nothing to hesitate for.
What would you have me call that, besides cowardice?

Though I hardly wished for it, the truth is that
the X Program is resolving the resource crisis.

Carefully observing the current situation may ultimately
lead to the least loss of life. We must think before we act—

We do not have time for such deliberate consideration! 
How many Digimon do you think have died while you sat around?

We wish to save all Digimon.
Which is why we must seize this opportunity.

Yes, we shall defeat that Digimon, Parallelmon,
and use their power to accomplish our goals!

Well, there they go.
I have to say, they were as stubborn as ever.

It isn't hard to understand their line of thinking.
It's the instinct of every living being to try to fend off a threat.

Even without a concrete, effective solution, and no matter
how low the odds, there's the urge to keep fighting.
It isn't logical at all, but feelings never are.

It almost sounds like you're talking about you and me,
although the threat we face is different.

By the way... what did they mean about using
Parallelmon's power to accomplish their goals?

That is as much a mystery to me as it is to you.
I suggest we track them down again and ask them.
We will all need to work together if we hope to return home...
